questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Gas' mechanism in Ethereum? (2-3 marks)
expectedPoints: Measures computational effort required for operations (e.g., transactions, smart contracts).
Prevents network abuse and infinite loops by attaching a cost to computation.
Compensates validators for the resources (CPU, memory) they expend.

questions.question: Compare and contrast Externally Owned Accounts (EOAs) and Contract Accounts (CAs) in Ethereum. (5-10 marks)
expectedPoints: Definition: EOA is controlled by a private key; CA is controlled by its code.
Creation: EOA is created from a private key; CA is created via a transaction.
Transaction Initiation: EOAs can initiate transactions; CAs cannot, they can only respond.
Code and Storage: EOAs have no code or storage; CAs have both code and storage.
Purpose: EOAs represent users; CAs represent deployed smart contracts and their state.
